meaning: adj.恐惧(或憎恨)外国人的,恐外的
examples: We have been traduced in the press as xenophobic bigots.
Service in the armed forces gave many Americans a less xenophobic view of the world.
The nation-state, though he was well aware of its parochial and xenophobic limitations, seemed to be liberty's best incubator.
But the popular anxiety that feeds such xenophobic movements is tempting ground for the centre.
The antagonist in my coming-out story was my grandmother, a woman as xenophobic as she was homophobic.
